blocks.text: Dear Ms. Herrera:

This letter confirms the terms on which {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} is retaining us for the executive messaging work we discussed, and it stands as our agreement once you sign it. It covers what we will do, who will do it, what it costs, and the terms we are both agreeing to. Please read it against the conversation we had. If a line here does not match what you expected, tell me before you sign it and not after. Executive messaging only works when the leader and the people drafting for that leader are saying the same thing, and getting that right starts with the two of us agreeing what this engagement is.
Scope of the Engagement

The work is executive messaging for {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}: a clear, consistent voice for your senior leaders in the settings that carry weight, and the language behind it that holds up from one appearance to the next. We are not rewriting what your executives believe or how they run the business. We are making sure that what they already stand for reaches the people who matter, in words that do not shift each time they are repeated.

Our method is unglamorous and it works. We listen first: to your leaders at Suarez-Jones, to the people who prepare them, and to the audiences who already carry an impression we may need to sharpen or correct. We reduce what we hear to a small set of core messages, written down, because a message that lives only in the room drifts the moment it leaves it. From there we build the material that carries those messages, and we stay with you when the messages are tested in public. A short position that stays put is worth more to you than a long one you spend the next year correcting.
The Team

John Macias will run this engagement day to day and is the person to bring anything that matters to. He carries only a few clients at a time, on purpose, so the one in front of him gets his attention, and he is the one who turns a question you raise into a next step you can see. When you want something moved, start with him.

Amy Carter will handle the drafting and keep each claim checked against its source before it reaches you. She is careful by habit and asks the question others skip, which is exactly what you want in the person holding the language. Between the two of them you will always know what is drafted, what is still open, and what needs you.

I lead the firm and I read what goes out under our name. I will not be on every call, but I am reachable, and if this engagement is not going the way you expected, I would rather hear it from you early than find it out late.
Fees and Payment

Our fee for the engagement is {{fact:f:E-2023-001.fee}}, set at the outset as a single figure rather than billed against hours. We work to a fixed fee so that our attention stays on the result, and so that the cost of a question never enters into whether you pick up the phone. We bill it in two equal parts: half as the work starts, and half once we reach the middle of the term. Approved out-of-pocket costs, such as travel to meet your leaders or the production of finished materials, are passed through at cost and listed line by line on each invoice. Invoices are due within thirty days.
Term and Termination

The engagement begins on {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}} and runs about five months, which is the basis on which we have planned it. Either of us may end it on fifteen days' written notice. If you end it, you owe the installments due to that date and the approved costs incurred, and nothing more. If we end it, which we would do only on reaching a point where we could not stand behind the work, we refund any fee already paid against work still undone.
Confidentiality

This work depends on discretion. We will treat your internal plans, your leaders' preparation, and the existence and content of this engagement as confidential, and we will use what you tell us about {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} that is not already public only to do the work you have hired us for, and we keep it inside the small group on each side that has to have it. We ask the same of you for our methods and our materials. Where an announcement carries its own timing, we take our cue from you.
Limitation of Liability

We will do this work with the care and skill our profession expects. How an audience takes a message, and how a given day moves, are not things we govern, so we do not promise a particular outcome; too much of what decides it sits outside anyone's hands. Our total liability under this engagement, on any basis, is limited to the fees you actually pay us for it, and neither of us is liable to the other for indirect or consequential loss. Nothing here limits liability for fraud or willful misconduct, nor should it.
Governing Law

This letter is the whole of our agreement on this engagement and replaces anything discussed or drafted before it. It is governed by the law of the state in which the firm keeps its principal office, and any dispute we cannot resolve between us goes to the courts sitting there, whose jurisdiction we each accept. Any change to these terms takes effect only in writing signed by both of us.
Acceptance

If this reflects our agreement, please sign below and return a copy to me. John Macias will follow up within the week to schedule the kickoff and to settle the first pieces of work, and Amy Carter will be in touch about the material she needs from your side. We are glad {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} chose us for this, and we intend to be worth the choice.
Sincerely,

We are on the executive messaging engagement for {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}}, live as of {{fact:f:E-2023-001.start}}. Jennifer Herrera, VP Finance, is our contact and holds sign-off on anything that goes outside the company. What we are producing: one steady voice for the Suarez-Jones leadership across the rooms and the pages that matter, and the language under it that reads the same from one quarter to the next. This memo is here to point us the same way before the work fans out.

The engagement runs a few months and the shape is the usual one: settle the framework, build the material around it, then hold the line through the first public tests of it. We are in the first stretch, so the near-term job is narrow. Get the framework and the fact base under it right, and everything downstream has something solid to stand on. A few practicals: billing and scheduling both run off the signed letter, outbound to the client goes through me, and the file stays current, because on this account the trail is mostly email and a thread nobody filed is a thread we have lost.
Workstreams

items: Message framework. Amy Carter takes the first draft: three or four core lines for the leadership voice, on paper rather than in anyone's head, back to the team for review inside two weeks. Keep it to a page. A leader who cannot recall the lines will not use them, and everything downstream leans on this, so it comes first.

Source pack. Amy Carter is building the fact base and tying each claim to a source before it reaches a draft, so nothing in a line depends on a number we have not checked. Any gap, bring it to me the day it shows up, not the week after. Chasing a figure now beats defending a wrong one later.

Approvals. External language routes to Jennifer Herrera for sign-off. I will settle with her who else on the client side reads language before it ships, so it reaches the right desks the first time and nothing goes outside ahead of the person who owns it. Where she wants a second reviewer, we build that into the schedule.

Operations. Julie Bailey holds the files, the calendar, and the mailbox, and marks where each draft stands in the approval chain. If Julie Bailey cannot find it in the file, we do not have it. One live draft per line, kept in the shared mailbox and not in anyone's personal inbox.

blocks.text: Cadence

Short internal check every Friday to keep the four workstreams lined up against each other. I will take a plain status to Jennifer Herrera on the same rhythm, so the client hears about a change from us rather than around us. If something is going to slip, say so early. Late notice is the expensive kind.
Next Steps

items: Amy Carter: framework draft to the team for internal review before a word of it travels to the client.
Julie Bailey: stand up the engagement file and set the billing and meeting cadence off the signed letter.
Me: lock the approval chain with Jennifer Herrera and get two or three windows from her for a first sit-down.

blocks.text: Keep the client channel narrow: anything headed to Jennifer Herrera or anyone else at Suarez-Jones comes through me. Thanks both.
To: Amy Carter
Amy,

Kicking off a single thread for the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} executive-messaging work, so status stays in one thread and nothing slips between us. Where things stand: the source pack is mostly assembled and I am partway through checking it against the documents Suarez-Jones sent over, the audience read is drafted, and there is a first cut of the leadership lines to push on.

Two things I need from you before we go further. First, take the source pack the rest of the way to verified this week, so we are not building lines on anything we cannot back. Second, pull together the specific questions only Jennifer Herrera can answer, and I will get them in front of her in one batch rather than piecemeal.

Once the framework is settled we should walk it together before it goes anywhere near the client, so we are not sending Jennifer Herrera anything we have not both read. Grab thirty minutes on my calendar later this week and we will go through it line by line.
John Macias

That split works. I will have the source pack finished by Thursday. Two figures still need a source before I will call them verified, and both sit on the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} revenue history rather than anything current, so they should not hold up the framework.

On the client questions, I have three so far that only Jennifer Herrera can settle, all on the Suarez-Jones side where the record runs thin. I will write them up tight for the single batch John Macias wants to send, so nothing goes over piecemeal. Thursday afternoon works to walk the framework once the pack is clean.
Amy Carter
Good. Send me the three questions whenever the pack is clean and I will get them to Jennifer Herrera the same day, batched as we said.

On Amy Carter's two open figures: if the sources do not turn up on the {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} revenue history, mark them unverified in the framework rather than dropping them, and we will close them with the Suarez-Jones finance team through Jennifer. Thursday afternoon is blocked on my calendar for the walk-through.
John Macias

Done. The pack is clean, the three questions are written up and in your inbox, and I marked the two {{fact:f:E-2023-001.client}} revenue figures as unverified in the framework the way John Macias asked rather than leaving them out.
Nothing else from me before Thursday. Once Jennifer Herrera comes back on the Suarez-Jones timeline, those are the last open items before the framework is ready for a first internal read.
Amy Carter
